channel.title: International Organisation Jobs (Brussels/NATO)
channel.description: Job listings for International Organisations roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Project Coordinator, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Project Coordinator support the coordination of project activities, stakeholder engagement, events, reporting, and programme development, contributing to the delivery of high-quality and impactful democratic governance interventions in close collaboration with the Project Manager, Ukraine. Duties and Responsibilities Project Coordination and Implementation Coordinates and ensures timely implementation of democratic governance activities, with a focus on electoral processes, parliamentary strengthening, and constitutional reform; Coordinates day-to-day implementation of project activities, including planning, scheduling, and follow-up on deliverables and milestones; Manages the contracting of consultants and service providers, including drafting TORs, reviewing deliverables, and ensuring compliance with quality and procurement standards; Coordinates with internal teams and technical advisors to ensure coherence and consistency across activities and alignment with international standards and comparative practices. Stakeholder Liaison and Representation Maintains regular communication and operational coordination with key national and international stakeholders, including electoral bodies, ministries, parliaments, civil society, and international partners; Prepares and delivers brief thematic presentations or updates during meetings, roundtables, and consultations as needed; Represents International IDEA at technical-level coordination platforms, including working groups, donor meetings, and stakeholder dialogues, as delegated by the supervisor. Project Development and Budgeting Contributes to the design of new initiatives, including drafting concept notes, identifying relevant interventions, and supporting the development of budgets; Supports donor reporting and proposal writing in collaboration with programme and finance teams; Implement programme activities in accordance with budget. Event and Mission Coordination Leads the substantive and logistical preparation of project-related events, consultative visits, stakeholder meetings, roundtables, and study visits; Oversees coordination with participants, speakers, venues, and logistics providers, ensuring smooth and professional delivery of events; Supports documentation of outcomes and preparation of summary reports from project activities. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Reporting Tracks the progress of activities against work plans, outputs, and indicators; Gathers inputs for internal and donor reporting, including drafting narrative sections of progress and final reports; Collects and systematizes evidence and feedback from consultations, missions, and workshops to inform outcome assessments and learning; Supports regular updates on the political and policy context affecting project delivery. Institutional Engagement and Knowledge Sharing Supports strategic engagement with EU institutions and international democracy support actors to advance programme objectives; Maintains up-to-date knowledge of trends in democratic governance and institutional reforms in Ukraine and the region; Coordinates inputs for knowledge products (e.g., lessons-learned papers, thematic briefs), ensuring the dissemination of project findings and good practices. General Profile Combines a good knowledge of the state of democracy and governance in Ukraine and the broader region, acquired through academic studies in political science, law, social science or related fields, and the relevant work experience; Will contribute significantly to assigned projects all along the project life cycle, i.e. from project design and funding to project assessment; will lead meetings and discussions, supervise publications and review input from external providers; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Adds value to team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model or mentor for less experienced colleagues; Can be expected to travel globally to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Project Manager - Electoral Preparedness and Integrity, Ukraine Education and Experience University degree in political science, law, international relations, public administration or other related discipline; Minimum of five (5) years of relevant experience in democracy and governance in Ukraine in the field of elections, political participation and representation, anti-corruption, rule of law or constitutionalism; Demonstrated experience working with Ukrainian national and foreign state authorities; Demonstrated experience in results-based report writing; Demonstrated experience in the implementation of projects in the field of democratic governance; Experience in resource mobilization, including conceptualization and formulation of successful funding proposals and knowledge of fundraising methods; Experience of working in an international context considered an advantage; Fluency in Ukrainian is required; Advanced spoken and written English is required. Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed-term appointment for one (1) year. Remuneration - USD 3 418 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) on 29 July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Project Manager, Electoral Preparedness and Integrity, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Project Manager will lead the design, planning and implementation of assigned projects and activities under International IDEA’s Ukraine programme portfolio, contributing to Ukraine’s democratic resilience, institutional recovery and reform agenda. Based in Kyiv, the incumbent will work as part of a larger Ukraine programme team based in Kyiv and Brussels, in close coordination with other Project Managers and programme staff, and will also work closely with the Central Election Commission of Ukraine, the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine, and other key national institutions and partners. Duties and Responsibilities Programme Management and Coordination Manages and coordinates assigned projects and activities under International IDEA’s Ukraine programme portfolio, with a focus on one or more thematic areas, including electoral assistance, ICT and cybersecurity, training, voter education, strategic communication, electoral risk management, parliamentary strengthening, constitution-building, political finance, foreign information manipulation and interference, regional development, and EU integration. The specific portfolio will be determined based on programme needs and the relevant experience of the successful candidate; Leads the design, planning, implementation, monitoring and learning of assigned projects and activities, ensuring timely, high-quality and results-oriented delivery; Develops and updates workplans, activity plans, timelines and implementation tools, and ensures effective day-to-day coordination with colleagues based in Kyiv, Brussels and HQ; Monitors relevant political, electoral, parliamentary, reform and socio-economic developments in Ukraine and contributes contextual and technical analysis to inform programme design and adaptive implementation; Identifies and manages operational, political, security and reputational risks related to assigned projects and activities. Project Development and Resource Mobilization Identifies emerging needs and programmatic opportunities in consultation with Ukrainian counterparts, International IDEA colleagues and partners; Contributes to the development of project concepts, concept notes and funding proposals in close collaboration with colleagues in Kyiv, Brussels and HQ; Supports donor engagement, communication and visibility related to assigned projects and contributes to resource mobilization efforts. Partnership Development Maintains and develops partnerships with key stakeholders in Ukraine, including the Central Election Commission, election administration bodies, the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine, relevant ministries and state institutions, academia, think tanks and civil society organizations; Fosters effective cooperation with International IDEA’s Member States, donor agencies, international organizations and democracy assistance actors based in Kyiv; Co-creates project interventions with national counterparts and partners to ensure relevance, ownership and sustainability. Representation and Thematic Interventions Represents International IDEA in meetings, coordination platforms, technical discussions, public events and high-level forums, as assigned; Delivers thematic presentations, contributes to policy dialogue and ensures International IDEA’s visibility in areas relevant to the assigned portfolio; Prepares thematic briefs, analytical notes, talking points, presentations and other technical inputs to support programme activities and external engagement. Procurement, Consultant and Partner Management Manages and/or coordinates the engagement of external consultants, experts, service providers and implementing partners, ensuring the quality and timely delivery of outputs; Prepares or contributes to terms of reference, technical specifications, procurement documentation and evaluation processes related to assigned activities; Ensures that procurement and service provision are carried out in accordance with International IDEA’s procurement policies and procedures and relevant donor requirements; Provides guidance to designated staff, consultants, interns and partners involved in the implementation of assigned projects and activities. Programme and Budget Planning Leads programme and budget planning for assigned portfolio components and contributes to broader Ukraine programme and regional planning processes; Monitors budget execution, expenditure planning and financial delivery of assigned projects in coordination with relevant finance, budget and programme performance colleagues; Ensures oversight of project funds in accordance with donor agreements, International IDEA regulations and approved workplans. Monitoring, Evaluation, Reporting and Learning Leads monitoring, evaluation, reporting and learning for assigned projects and activities, ensuring alignment with International IDEA’s results-based management system; Tracks progress against outputs, outcomes, indicators and donor requirements, and contributes to programme-related progress reports; Identifies lessons learned, good practices and opportunities for adaptive programming and knowledge sharing across the Ukraine programme. General Profile Combines in-depth knowledge, acquired through post-graduate academic achievements, excellent skills and significant experience in their field; Combines in-depth knowledge in current democratization trends and challenges, especially in Ukraine, as well as in the broader Eastern European region, with a particular focus on democratic governance, electoral processes, parliamentary development, constitutional reforms, FIMI, political finance and democracy assessment frameworks; Manages assigned projects throughout the project life cycle, i.e. from strategic planning and project design, funding and budgeting to project assessment and accountability; leads meetings and discussions, supervises publications and input from external providers; Has contributed to publications on their relevant subject; is recognized as a knowledge resource in their broad professional community; Has the intellectual leadership to integrate innovation into their field of expertise; Fosters team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model and mentor for colleagues; Is expected to travel globally to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Ukraine Programme Lead Education and Experience University degree, preferably post graduate in political science, law, international relations, public administration or other related discipline; Minimum of eight (8) years of relevant experience in strengthening democracy and accountability in Ukraine; Experienced in the fields of electoral assistance, parliamentary support, FIMI as well as other rule of law and human rights-related fields in Ukraine; Demonstrated experience in conceptualization, formulation and management of project activities; Experience in fundraising and project design is essential; Demonstrated experience in report writing and reporting under a results-based management (RBM) system; Experience of working in an international context considered an advantage. Fluency in written and spoken English and Ukrainian is required.; Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment for one (1) year. Remuneration - USD 4 570 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. T
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Senior Finance and Operations Officer, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Senior Finance and Operations Officer supports the financial management of International IDEA’s Ukraine portfolio by overseeing budgeting, forecasting, expenditure monitoring, financial reporting, reconciliations and financial record-keeping. The role also provides financial oversight of procurement, contracting and partner-related processes, ensuring compliance with International IDEA policies, donor requirements and contractual obligations. Duties and Responsibilities Financial Management Manages the supplier ledger, including processing invoices and payments, maintaining supplier accounts, recording transactions and ensuring that all expenditures are properly supported and authorized; Prepares and monitors project budgets, expenditure forecasts and cash-flow projections; analyze budget utilization; identify variances; and support budget revisions and remittance requests; Works closely with programme and administrative colleagues to ensure that activities financed from restricted funds are properly planned, implemented, documented and reported; Prepares monthly and periodic financial reports, reconciles bank accounts and petty cash, and maintain accurate records in the centralized accounting and ERP systems; Monitors programme expenditure and financial commitments to ensure compliance with International IDEA policies, donor requirements, approved budgets and applicable Ukrainian legislation; Reviews financial documentation relating to meetings, workshops, travel and other programme activities, including cost estimates, advances, claims, invoices and supporting documentation; Supports the opening and ongoing administration of International IDEA’s bank accounts in Ukraine, including preparation of required documentation, liaison with banks, maintenance of authorized signatory records and resolution of operational banking issues; Performs any other related financial and administrative duties assigned within the scope of the position. Contracts, Compliance and Audit Support Supports the preparation, financial administration and monitoring of contracts and agreements with service providers, consultants and partner organizations, in coordination with the Procurement Officer; Prepares and maintain complete financial, contractual and audit files, and support project, donor and institutional audits and follow-up actions; Coordinates and support International IDEA’s registration and ongoing compliance with relevant Ukrainian authorities, including tax, statistical, employment, social security and other mandatory registrations, notifications and filings; Maintains an up-to-date register of statutory obligations, registration documents, certificates, deadlines and reporting requirements, and coordinate timely submissions, renewals and amendments; Monitors changes in Ukrainian financial, banking and administrative requirements relevant to the Ukraine Office and advise management on any necessary actions; Office Administration and Operations Manages the day-to-day administrative and operational functioning of the Ukraine Office, ensuring that office facilities, equipment, utilities and administrative services are maintained and operate effectively; Liaises with landlords, building management, service providers and other vendors to support the maintenance, safety and functionality of office premises; Coordinates the procurement, inventory management and maintenance of office supplies, equipment, furniture and other administrative resources required for the effective operation of the Ukraine Office; Monitors office-related expenditure and maintain accurate records of equipment, furniture and other assets procured for the Ukraine Office; Liaises with Ukrainian authorities, banks, auditors, payroll providers, legal advisers and other service providers on financial, banking, statutory and administrative matters; General Profile Analyzes performance and identifies issues and trends affecting project funding; recommends the use of appropriate programme tools and strategies; Develops financial proposals and technical tools to address identified programme needs in the specialized area of project funds accounting; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Adds value to team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other departments of the regional office; Acts as a model or mentor for less experienced colleagues; Can be expected to travel to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Ukraine Programme Lead. Education and Experience University degree in financial management, business management or equivalent; Full or part professional qualifications in finance and accounting, for example ACCA, CPA, CIMA , CA or equivalent is considered an advantage; A minimum of five (5) years of professional experience in financial management, grant management, and project budgeting and reporting; Experience working in multi-donor environments considered an advantage; Knowledge of award and contract regulations and reporting requirements of EU and/or other major donors considered and advantage; Experience in working in an international context, e.g. in an intergovernmental organization, considered an advantage; Experience using ERP systems (particularly Unit4) considered and advantage. Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed-term appointment until 31 December 2027 Remuneration - USD 3 418 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) 31 July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Voter Education Officer, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Voter Education Officer will support the Central Election Commission of Ukraine in strengthening its capacity to communicate effectively with voters and stakeholders, and in the development and implementation of voter education, public awareness and information campaigns aimed at promoting informed and inclusive participation in democratic processes. The incumbent supports programme implementation through technical advice, voter education and communication materials, stakeholder engagement, public outreach, and activities related to social media monitoring, information integrity and resilience against foreign information manipulation and interference (FIMI). The role also supports the communication and visibility of International IDEA’s work in Ukraine through content development, media engagement, events and digital communications, in close coordination with Headquarters. Duties and Responsibilities Communication Capacity Strengthening, Voter Education and Information Integrity Provides technical expertise and implementation support aimed at strengthening the communication, public outreach and voter education capacity of the Central Election Commission of Ukraine and other relevant electoral stakeholders; Supports the Project Manager in the development of voter education approaches, communication strategies, public information initiatives and outreach campaigns related to electoral processes, democratic participation, electoral integrity and institutional resilience with a special focus on marginalized groups (women, people with disabilities, internally displaced people, refugees, veterans, etc.); Advises on and supports the design, testing and implementation of voter education and public information materials, tools and campaigns aimed at improving public understanding of electoral processes, promoting informed participation and strengthening trust in democratic institutions; Supports programme activities aimed at addressing misinformation, information disorder and foreign information manipulation and interference (FIMI), including through voter education, strategic communication and public awareness initiatives; Contributes to the development of methodologies, guidance materials, analytical products and practical recommendations related to voter education, electoral communication, public outreach, information integrity and resilience; Supports social media monitoring and analysis activities, including the identification of voter information needs, communication trends, emerging risks and issues relevant to electoral communication and information integrity; Prepares analytical summaries, briefing notes, communication materials and technical recommendations for electoral stakeholders, programme partners and International IDEA; Coordinates and oversees the work of consultants, service providers and implementing partners engaged in voter education, communication, public outreach, social media monitoring and related activities; Contributes to the visibility and communication of International IDEA’s activities in Ukraine through the preparation of communication content, event materials, success stories and other outreach products, in coordination with International IDEA’s Communications Team. Cooperation and Stakeholder Engagement Works in close coordination with International IDEA’s Communications Team at Headquarters to ensure that communication products and visibility activities are aligned with organizational standards, messaging and branding requirements; Establishes, coordinates and maintains effective working relationships with electoral management bodies, state institutions, civil society organizations, media-related stakeholders, academic institutions and other relevant actors engaged in voter education, communication and information integrity; Provides technical advice and support to programme teams, consultants and partners, and contributes expertise on voter education, public outreach and information integrity to programme design, implementation and adaptive management processes; Represents International IDEA in meetings, consultations, conferences, working groups and stakeholder engagements related to voter education, electoral communication, public outreach and information integrity, as required. General Profile Combines in-depth knowledge, acquired through post-graduate academic achievements, excellent skills and significant experience in their field; Combines in-depth knowledge in current democratization trends and challenges, especially in Ukraine, with a particular focus on democratic governance, electoral processes, parliamentary development, constitutional reforms, FIMI, political finance and democracy assessment frameworks. Contributes to assigned projects throughout the project life cycle, i.e. from strategic planning and project design, funding and budgeting to project assessment and accountability; supports meetings and discussions, reviews publications and input from external providers; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Adds value to team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model or mentor for less experienced colleagues; Can be expected to travel globally to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Project Manager, Democratic Resilience and Capacity Building Education and Experience University degree in communications, journalism, political science, public administration, social sciences or another relevant field; Minimum five (5) years of relevant professional experience in strategic communication, public information, voter education, media relations, institutional communication or related fields, preferably within international organizations, public institutions, electoral bodies or donor-funded projects; Experience producing high-quality communication products and supporting communication campaigns, public outreach, voter education or related initiatives for diverse audiences and platforms; Experience in communications related to democratic governance, elections, public institutions or civic participation; Experience engaging with a range of stakeholders, including government institutions, electoral bodies, media, UN entities, NGOs and other partners, is considered an advantage; Experience of working in an international context, e.g. in an intergovernmental organization, is considered an advantage; Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment until 31 Dec 2027. Remuneration - USD 3 418 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) 04 August 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email on
item.dc:creator: International IDEA
